(3) (3) NH3 > CN⁻ > Br⁻ > C6H5⁻
(4) (4) CN⁻ > C6H5⁻ > Br⁻ > NH3. ✓ Correct
Solution: Trans effect order - - - > > > CN C H Br NH 653
Q141 | Inorganic Chemistry: d & f-block Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following statements related to lanthanons is incorrect ?
(1) (1) All the lanthanons are much more reactive than aluminium ✓ Correct
(2) (2) Ce(+4) solutions are widely used as oxidizing agent in volumetric analysis
(3) (3) Europium shows +2 oxidation state.
(4) (4) The basicity decreases as the ionic radius decreases from Pr to Lu.
Solution: (1) Lanthanon's are less reactive than aluminium due to high IP (Lanthanoid contraction) (2) Ce⁺⁴ is good oxidising agent and easily converted into Ce+3 (3) Eu(63) = 4f7 5d⁰ 6s², Eu+2 = 4f7 (4) In lanthanoids series 'Ce' to Lu ionic radius regular decreases and covalent character increase, basic character of hydroxide decrease
Q142 | Inorganic Chemistry: Coordination Chemistry Medium
ok conf:1.00
Jahn-Teller effect not observed in high spin complexes of :
(1) (1) d4
(2) (2) d⁹
(3) (3) d⁷
(4) (4) d⁸ ✓ Correct
Solution: John Teller effect explain axial distortion in unsymmetrical octahedral geometry. It is not found in high spin complexes of d⁸.
Q143 | Organic Chemistry: Hydrocarbons Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following can be used as the halide component for Friedel-Crafts reaction?
(1) (1) Chloroethene
(2) (2) Isopropyl chloride ✓ Correct
(3) (3) Chlorobenzene
(4) (4) Bromobenzene
Solution: Isopropyl chloride (a haloalkane) can serve as the halide component for Friedel-Crafts alkylation. Chloroethene and halobenzenes are less reactive due to partial double bond character from resonance, which hinders electrophilic attack for alkylation. Friedel-Crafts alkylation requires a good carbocation source.
[solution diagram]

Q144 | Organic Chemistry: Stereochemistry (Isomerism) Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
In which of the following molecules, all atoms are coplanar ?
(1) CH2=C(CN)2
[option 1]


(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: In biphenyl (option 3), the two phenyl rings can rotate around the single bond, allowing for a coplanar conformation where all atoms lie in the same plane. The other options contain non-planar cyclic structures (cyclohexane, cyclobutane) or functional groups (allene in option 1) that enforce non-planarity for all atoms.
[solution diagram]

Q145 | Organic Chemistry: Polymer Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following structures represents nylon 6,6 polymer ?
(1) [DIAGRAM]
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: Nylon 6,6 is a polyamide formed by the condensation polymerization of hexamethylenediamine (H2N-(CH2)6-NH2) and adipic acid (HOOC-(CH2)4-COOH), each having 6 carbon atoms. Option (2) correctly depicts the repeating unit formed from these two monomers.
[solution diagram]

Q146 | Organic Chemistry: Amines Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
In pyrrole [DIAGRAM] The electron density is maximum on :
[question diagram]

(1) (1) 2 and 4
(2) (2) 2 and 5 ✓ Correct
(3) (3) 2 and 3
(4) (4) 3 and 4
Solution: Pyrrole is an aromatic heterocyclic compound where the lone pair on nitrogen participates in resonance with the ring. Resonance structures show that the electron density is highest at positions 2 and 5 of the ring (α-positions to nitrogen), making them most susceptible to electrophilic attack.
[solution diagram]

Q147 | Organic Chemistry: Hydrocarbons Harddiagram
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following compounds shall not produced propene by reaction with HBr followed by elimination of direct only elimination reaction ?
(1) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: Option (1) is ketene (CH2=C=O). Addition of HBr to ketene forms bromoacetyl bromide (CH2BrCOBr). Subsequent elimination from this compound would not directly yield propene. The other options, propan-1-ol, propan-2-ol, and 1-bromopropane, can all readily form propene via elimination after reaction with HBr (if not already a haloalkane).
[solution diagram]

Q148 | Organic Chemistry: Amines Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following nitro-compounds does not react with nitrous acid ?
[question diagram]




(1) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: Tertiary nitro compounds (like the one in option 1, 2-methyl-2-nitropropane) do not have any α-hydrogens. The reaction of nitro compounds with nitrous acid typically involves the α-hydrogens, hence tertiary nitro compounds do not react with HNO2.
Q149 | Organic Chemistry: Biomolecules Easydiagram
ok conf:1.00
The central dogma of molecular genetics states that the genetic information flows from:
(1) (1) DNA → RNA → Proteins ✓ Correct
(2) (2) DNA → RNA → Carbohydrates
(3) (3) Amino acids → Proteins
(4) (4) DNA → Carbohydrates → Proteins
Solution: The central dogma of molecular biology describes the flow of genetic information in cells from DNA to RNA (transcription) and then from RNA to protein (translation).
Q150 | Organic Chemistry: Biomolecules Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
The correct corresponding order names of four aldoses with configuration given below [DIAGRAM]
[question diagram]

(1) (1) L-erythrose, L-threose, D-erythrose, D-threose
(2) (2) D-erythrose, D-threose, L-erythrose, L-threose ✓ Correct
(3) (3) L-erythrose, L-threose, L-erythrose, D-threose
(4) (4) D-threose, D-erythrose, L-threose, L-erythrose
Solution: D/L designation is based on the configuration of the chiral center farthest from the aldehyde group; if -OH is on the right, it's D. Erythrose has two adjacent chiral centers with the same configuration, while threose has opposite. Based on this, the structures correspond to D-erythrose, D-threose, L-erythrose, and L-threose respectively.
[solution diagram]

Q151 | Organic Chemistry: Aromatic Hydrocarbons Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
In the given reaction : [DIAGRAM] The product P is:
(1) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM]
[option 4]

Solution: The reaction shown is a Friedel-Crafts alkylation reaction of benzene with HF as the alkylating agent in the presence of 0°C. Benzene reacts with HF to form fluorobenzene as the main product through electrophilic aromatic substitution, involving the formation of a carbocation intermediate.
[solution diagram]

Q152 | Organic Chemistry: N-containing Compound Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
A given nitrogen-containing aromatic compound A reacts with Sn/HCl, followed by HNO2 to give an unstable compound B. B, on treatment with phenol, forms a beautiful coloured compound C with the molecular formula C12H10N2O. The structure of compound A is :
(1) [DIAGRAM]
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 4]

Solution: The sequence of reactions—reduction with Sn/HCl (nitro to amino), then diazotization with HNO2 (amino to diazonium salt), followed by coupling with phenol (forming an azo dye with formula C12H10N2O)—indicates that the starting compound A is nitrobenzene.
[solution diagram]

Q153 | Organic Chemistry: Haloalkanes & Haloarenes Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
Consider the reaction CH3CH2CH2Br + NaCN → CH3CH2CH2CN + NaBr This reaction will be the fastest in :
(1) (1) N, N’-dimethylformamide (DMF) ✓ Correct
(2) (2) Water
(3) (3) Ethanol
(4) (4) Methanol
Solution: The reaction is an SN2 reaction. SN2 reactions are favored by polar aprotic solvents like N,N'-dimethylformamide (DMF), which solvate cations effectively but leave anions (nucleophiles) relatively unsolvated and more reactive. Protic solvents (water, ethanol, methanol) would solvate the nucleophile and slow down the SN2 reaction.
Q154 | Organic Chemistry: Aldehyde & Ketones Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
The correct structure of the product A formed in the reaction : [DIAGRAM]
[question diagram]






(1) [DIAGRAM]
[option 1]

(2) [DIAGRAM]
[option 2]

(3) [DIAGRAM]
[option 3]

(4) [DIAGRAM] ✓ Correct
[option 4]

Solution: The reaction shows the catalytic hydrogenation of cyclohexanone (a ketone) using H2 in the presence of Pd/Carbon and ethanol. This process reduces the carbonyl group (C=O) of the ketone to a secondary alcohol. Therefore, the product A is cyclohexanol.
[solution diagram]

Q155 | Organic Chemistry: Isomerism Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
Which among the given molecules can exhibit tautomerism ? [DIAGRAM]
[question diagram]



(1) (1) Both I and II
(2) (2) Both II and III
(3) (3) III only ✓ Correct
(4) (4) Both I and III
Solution: Tautomerism, specifically keto-enol tautomerism, requires the presence of an alpha-hydrogen (a hydrogen on a carbon adjacent to a carbonyl group). Compound III (a β-dicarbonyl compound) has acidic α-hydrogens between the two carbonyl groups, allowing it to undergo keto-enol tautomerism. Compounds I and II lack such α-hydrogens.
[solution diagram]

Q156 | Organic Chemistry: Carboxylic Acids Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
The correct order of strengths of the carboxylic acids [DIAGRAM] Is :
[question diagram]

(1) (1) III > II > I
(2) (2) II > I > III
(3) (3) I > II > III
(4) (4) II > III > I ✓ Correct
Solution: The acidity of carboxylic acids increases with the presence of electron-withdrawing groups (-I effect) and decreases with electron-donating groups (+I effect). Comparing the structures, compound II has a strongly activating amine group, compound III has a less activating methyl group, and compound I has no additional substituents. The provided structures imply a specific order. If the structures are: I (benzoic acid), II (p-nitrobenzoic acid), III (p-methylbenzoic acid), then the order would be II>I>III. However, assuming they are substituted acetic acids and considering the solution diagram i...
[solution diagram]

Q157 | Organic Chemistry: Hydrocarbons Mediumdiagram
ok conf:1.00
The compound that will react most readily with gaseous bromine has the formula :
[question diagram]

(1) (1) C4H10
(2) (2) C2H4
(3) (3) C3H6 ✓ Correct
(4) (4) C2H2
Solution: Propene (C3H6) is an alkene. Alkenes undergo electrophilic addition reactions with bromine water (decolorizing it). However, the solution mentions "allylic product by free radical mechanism", which is characteristic of reaction with gaseous bromine at high temperatures or under UV light (NBS type conditions). Among the given options, propene has allylic hydrogens that are readily abstracted by a bromine radical, leading to facile allylic bromination via a free radical mechanism.
Q158 | Inorganic Chemistry: Hydrogen Bonding Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following compounds shows the presence of intramolecular hydrogen bond ?
(1) (1) Cellulose ✓ Correct
(2) (2) Concentrated acetic acid
(3) (3) H2O2
(4) (4) HCN
Solution: Cellulose is a polysaccharide with multiple hydroxyl groups. Its structure permits extensive intramolecular hydrogen bonding between -OH groups within the same chain, contributing to its fibrous nature and insolubility. The other compounds primarily exhibit intermolecular hydrogen bonding.
Q159 | Physical Chemistry: Electrochemistry Medium
ok conf:1.00
The molar conductivity of a 0.5 mol/dm³ solution of AgNO3 with electrolytic conductivity of 5.76 × 10⁻³ S cm⁻¹ at 298 K is :
(1) (1) 0.086 S cm²/mol
(2) (2) 28.8 S cm²/mol
(3) (3) 2.88 S cm²/mol
(4) (4) 11.52 S cm²/mol ✓ Correct
Solution: Molar conductivity (Λm) is calculated as Λm = (κ × 1000) / C, where κ is the specific conductivity in S cm⁻¹ and C is the concentration in mol/L. Given κ = 5.76 × 10⁻³ S cm⁻¹ and C = 0.5 mol/dm³ = 0.5 mol/L. So, Λm = (5.76 × 10⁻³ S cm⁻¹ × 1000 cm³ L⁻¹) / 0.5 mol L⁻¹ = 11.52 S cm² mol⁻¹.
Q160 | Physical Chemistry: Chemical Kinetics Medium
ok conf:1.00
The decomposition of phosphine (PH3) on tungsten at low pressure is a first-order reaction. It is because the :
(1) (1) Rate is independent of the surface coverage
(2) (2) Rate of decomposition is very slow
(3) (3) Rate is proportional to the surface coverage ✓ Correct
(4) (4) Rate is inversely proportional to the surface coverage
Solution: For a surface-catalyzed reaction at low pressures, the surface sites are largely available, and the rate of reaction is directly proportional to the concentration of the reactant molecules adsorbed on the surface, which is proportional to the gas-phase concentration. This makes the reaction appear first-order with respect to the gas-phase concentration.
Q161 | Physical Chemistry: Surface Chemistry Easy
ok conf:1.00
The coagulation values in millimoles per litre of the electrolytes used for the coagulation of As2S3 are given below : I. (NaCl) = 52, II. (BaCl2) = 0.69, III. (MgSO4) = 0.22 The correct order of their coagulating power is :
(1) (1) III > II > I ✓ Correct
(2) (2) III > I > II
(3) (3) I > II > II
(4) (4) II > I > II
Solution: Coagulating power is inversely proportional to the coagulation value (flocculation value). Since As2S3 is a negatively charged sol, the coagulating power depends on the valency of the cation (Hardy-Schulze rule). MgSO4 provides Mg²⁺, BaCl2 provides Ba²⁺, and NaCl provides Na⁺. The coagulation values are MgSO4 (0.22) < BaCl2 (0.69) < NaCl (52). Therefore, the coagulating power order is MgSO4 > BaCl2 > NaCl, which corresponds to III > II > I.
Q162 | Physical Chemistry: Electrochemistry Medium
ok conf:1.00
During the electrolysis of molten sodium chloride, the time required to produce 0.10 mol of chlorine gas using a current of 3 amperes is :
(1) (1) 220 minutes
(2) (2) 330 minutes
(3) (3) 55 minutes
(4) (4) 110 minutes ✓ Correct
Solution: The reaction for chlorine gas production is 2Cl⁻ → Cl₂(g) + 2e⁻. To produce 1 mole of Cl₂, 2 moles of electrons (2 Faraday) are required. For 0.10 mol of Cl₂, 0.20 moles of electrons are needed. Total charge (Q) = n × F = 0.20 mol × 96485 C/mol ≈ 19297 C. Using Q = I × t, we get t = Q / I = 19297 C / 3 A ≈ 6432.33 seconds. Converting to minutes: 6432.33 / 60 ≈ 107.2 minutes, which is approximately 110 minutes.
Q163 | Physical Chemistry: Atomic Structure Easy
ok conf:1.00
How many electrons can fit in the orbital for which n = 3 and l =1 ?
(1) (1) 10
(2) (2) 14
(3) (3) 2 ✓ Correct
(4) (4) 6
Solution: For quantum numbers n=3 and l=1, this specifies a 3p orbital. According to the Pauli exclusion principle, any single orbital can hold a maximum of two electrons, provided they have opposite spins.
Q164 | Physical Chemistry: Thermodynamics Medium
ok conf:1.00
For a sample of perfect gas when its pressure is changed isothermally from pi to pf, the entropy change is given by :
(1) (1) ΔS = nRT In (P_f / P_i)
(2) (2) ΔS = RT In (P_f / P_i)
(3) (3) ΔS = nR In (P_i / P_f) ✓ Correct
(4) (4) ΔS = nR In (P_f / P_i)
Solution: For an isothermal process (constant temperature, dT=0) involving an ideal gas, the entropy change is given by ΔS = nR ln(V_f/V_i). Since for an ideal gas at constant temperature, P_iV_i = P_fV_f, we have V_f/V_i = P_i/P_f. Substituting this into the entropy change equation gives ΔS = nR ln(P_i/P_f).
Q165 | Physical Chemistry: Solution Easy
ok conf:1.00
The van't Hoff factor (i) for a dilute aqueous solution of the strong electrolyte barium hydroxide is :
(1) (1) 2
(2) (2) 3 ✓ Correct
(3) (3) 0
(4) (4) 1
Solution: Barium hydroxide, Ba(OH)2, is a strong electrolyte, meaning it dissociates completely in water. The dissociation equation is Ba(OH)2(aq) → Ba²⁺(aq) + 2OH⁻(aq). For every one formula unit of Ba(OH)2, three ions are produced (one Ba²⁺ and two OH⁻). Therefore, the van't Hoff factor (i) for Ba(OH)2 is 3.
Q166 | Physical Chemistry: Ionic Equilibrium Medium
ok conf:1.00
The percentage of pyridine (C5H5N) that forms pyridinium ion (C5H5N⁺H) in a 0.10 M aqueous pyridine solution (Kb for C5H5N = 1.7 × 10⁻⁹) is :
(1) (1) 0.77%
(2) (2) 1.6%
(3) (3) 0.0060%
(4) (4) 0.013% ✓ Correct
Solution: Pyridine (C5H5N) is a weak base, so its dissociation in water can be represented as C5H5N + H2O ⇌ C5H5N⁺H + OH⁻. The degree of dissociation (α) for a weak base is given by α = √(Kb/C). Given Kb = 1.7 × 10⁻⁹ and C = 0.10 M. So, α = √(1.7 × 10⁻⁹ / 0.10) = √(1.7 × 10⁻⁸) ≈ 1.3038 × 10⁻⁴. The percentage of pyridine that forms pyridinium ion is (α × 100)% = (1.3038 × 10⁻⁴ × 100)% ≈ 0.013%.
Q167 | Physical Chemistry: Solid State Easy
ok conf:1.00
In calcium fluoride, having the fluorite structure, the coordination numbers for calcium ion (Ca²⁺) and fluoride ion (F⁻) are :
(1) (1) 8 and 4 ✓ Correct
(2) (2) 4 and 8
(3) (3) 4 and 2
(4) (4) 6 and 6
Solution: In the fluorite (CaF2) crystal structure, the Ca²⁺ ions form a face-centered cubic (FCC) lattice, and the F⁻ ions occupy all the tetrahedral voids. Each Ca²⁺ ion is surrounded by 8 F⁻ ions, and each F⁻ ion is surrounded by 4 Ca²⁺ ions. Thus, the coordination number for Ca²⁺ is 8 and for F⁻ is 4.
Q168 | Physical Chemistry: Electrochemistry Easy
ok conf:1.00
If the E_cell for a given reaction has a negative value, which of the following gives the correct relationships for the values of ΔG° and K_eq?
[question diagram]


(1) (1) ΔG° < 0; K_eq > 1
(2) (2) ΔG° < 0; K_eq < 1
(3) (3) ΔG° > 0; K_eq < 1 ✓ Correct
(4) (4) ΔG° > 0; K_eq > 1
Solution: The relationship between standard cell potential (E°cell), standard Gibbs free energy change (ΔG°), and equilibrium constant (Keq) are given by ΔG° = -nFE°cell and ΔG° = -RT lnKeq. If E°cell has a negative value, then ΔG° = -nF(-ve) = +ve, so ΔG° > 0. If ΔG° > 0, then -RT lnKeq > 0, which implies lnKeq < 0, and therefore Keq < 1.
Q169 | Physical Chemistry: Liquid Solution Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following is incorrect for ideal solution ?
(1) (1) ΔP = P_observed - P_calculated by Raoult's law = 0
(2) (2) ΔG_mix = 0 ✓ Correct
(3) (3) ΔH_mix = 0
(4) (4) ΔU_mix = 0
Solution: For an ideal solution, the enthalpy of mixing (ΔHmix) is zero, the volume of mixing (ΔVmix) is zero, and it obeys Raoult's law (ΔP = 0). Since mixing is a spontaneous process, the Gibbs free energy of mixing (ΔGmix) must be negative (ΔGmix < 0), not zero. Thus, ΔGmix = 0 is an incorrect statement for an ideal solution.
Q170 | Physical Chemistry: Ionic Equilibrium Medium
ok conf:1.00
The solubility of AgCl(s) with solubility product 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰ in 0.1 M NaCl solution would be :
[question diagram]


(1) (1) 1.6 × 10⁻¹¹ M
(2) (2) Zero
(3) (3) 1.26 × 10⁻⁵ M
(4) (4) 1.6 × 10⁻⁹ M ✓ Correct
Solution: For the dissolution of AgCl: AgCl(s) ⇌ Ag⁺(aq) + Cl⁻(aq). Let the solubility of AgCl in 0.1 M NaCl be S. So, [Ag⁺] = S. The initial concentration of Cl⁻ from NaCl is 0.1 M, so total [Cl⁻] = 0.1 + S. Ksp = [Ag⁺][Cl⁻] = S(0.1 + S). Since Ksp (1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰) is very small, we can assume S << 0.1, so (0.1 + S) ≈ 0.1. Thus, 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰ = S × 0.1, which gives S = 1.6 × 10⁻⁹ M.
Q171 | Physical Chemistry: Mole Concept Medium
ok conf:1.00
Suppose the elements X and Y combine to form two compounds XY2 and X3Y2. When 0.1 mole of XY2 weighs 10 g and 0.05 mole of X3Y2 weighs 9 g, the atomic weights of X and Y are :
(1) (1) 20, 30
(2) (2) 30, 20
(3) (3) 40, 30 ✓ Correct
(4) (4) 60, 40
Solution: Let Ax and Ay be the atomic weights of X and Y. For XY2: 0.1 mol = 10 g, so molar mass (M) = 10/0.1 = 100 g/mol. Thus, Ax + 2Ay = 100 (Eq 1). For X3Y2: 0.05 mol = 9 g, so M = 9/0.05 = 180 g/mol. Thus, 3Ax + 2Ay = 180 (Eq 2). Subtracting (Eq 1) from (Eq 2): (3Ax + 2Ay) - (Ax + 2Ay) = 180 - 100 => 2Ax = 80 => Ax = 40. Substituting Ax = 40 into (Eq 1): 40 + 2Ay = 100 => 2Ay = 60 => Ay = 30. So, atomic weights are 40 for X and 30 for Y.
Q172 | Physical Chemistry: Electrochemistry Easy
ok conf:1.00
The number of electrons delivered at the cathode during electrolysis by a current of 1 ampere in 60 seconds (charge on electron = 1.60 × 10⁻¹⁹ C)
(1) (1) 3.75 × 10²⁰ ✓ Correct
(2) (2) 7.48 × 10²³
(3) (3) 6 × 10²³
(4) (4) 6 × 10²⁰
Solution: The total charge (Q) delivered is Q = I × t = 1 ampere × 60 seconds = 60 Coulombs. The number of electrons (n) is found by dividing the total charge by the charge of a single electron (e): n = Q / e = 60 C / (1.60 × 10⁻¹⁹ C/electron) = 3.75 × 10²⁰ electrons.
Q173 | Inorganic Chemistry: p-block Easy
ok conf:1.00
Boric acid is an acid because its molecule :
[question diagram]

(1) (1) Accepts OH⁻ from water releasing proton ✓ Correct
(2) (2) Combines with proton from water molecule
(3) (3) Contains replaceable H⁺ ion
(4) (4) Gives up a proton
Solution: Boric acid (H3BO3 or B(OH)3) acts as a weak Lewis acid. It does not donate a proton directly but rather accepts a hydroxide ion (OH⁻) from water, thereby releasing a proton (H⁺) from the water molecule. The reaction is B(OH)3 + H2O ⇌ [B(OH)4]⁻ + H⁺.
Q174 | Coordination Compound Easy
ok conf:1.00
AlF3 is soluble in HF only in presence of KF. It is due to the formation of :
(1) (1) AlH3
(2) (2) K[AlF3H]
(3) (3) K3[AlF3H3]
(4) (4) K3[AlF6] ✓ Correct
Solution: Aluminum fluoride (AlF3) is sparingly soluble in HF alone. However, in the presence of potassium fluoride (KF), additional fluoride ions (F⁻) are available to react with AlF3 to form a soluble complex ion, [AlF6]³⁻. This complex is formed as K3[AlF6], which is a soluble potassium hexafluoroaluminate.
Q175 | Physical Chemistry: Electrochemistry Easy
ok conf:1.00
Zinc can be coated on iron to produce galvanized iron but the reverse is not possible. It is because :
(1) (1) Zinc has lower negative electrode potential than iron
(2) (2) Zinc has higher negative electrode potential than iron ✓ Correct
(3) (3) Zinc is lighter than iron
(4) (4) Zinc has lower melting point than iron
Solution: Galvanization relies on sacrificial protection. Zinc has a higher negative standard electrode potential (E° = -0.76 V) compared to iron (E° = -0.44 V). This means zinc is more easily oxidized than iron and will corrode preferentially when in contact, thus protecting the iron. The reverse process (iron coating zinc) is not effective because iron is less reactive than zinc.
Q176 | Physical Chemistry: Surface Chemistry Easy
ok conf:1.00
The suspension of slaked lime in water is known as :
(1) (1) Milk of lime ✓ Correct
(2) (2) Aqueous solution of slaked lime
(3) (3) Limewater
(4) (4) Quicklime
Solution: Slaked lime is calcium hydroxide, Ca(OH)2. A suspension of Ca(OH)2 in water is commonly known as milk of lime, due to its milky appearance. A clear, saturated aqueous solution of Ca(OH)2 is called limewater.
Q177 | Inorganic Chemistry: Chemical Bonding Easy
ok conf:1.00
The hybridizations of atomic orbital of nitrogen in NO₂, NO₃⁻ and NH₄⁺ respectively are
(1) (1) sp, sp² and sp³ ✓ Correct
(2) (2) sp², sp and sp³
(3) (3) sp, sp³ and sp²
(4) (4) sp², sp³ and sp
Solution: For NO2, nitrogen is sp hybridized (linear molecule with one unpaired electron). For NO3⁻ (nitrate ion), nitrogen is sp² hybridized (trigonal planar geometry). For NH4⁺ (ammonium ion), nitrogen is sp³ hybridized (tetrahedral geometry). Therefore, the hybridizations are sp, sp², and sp³ respectively.
Q178 | Inorganic Chemistry: Chemical Bonding Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following fluoro-compounds is most likely to behave as a Lewis base ?
(1) (1) CF4
(2) (2) SiF4
(3) (3) BF3
(4) (4) PF3 ✓ Correct
Solution: A Lewis base is a species that can donate an electron pair. BF3 is an electron-deficient compound and acts as a Lewis acid. CF4 and SiF4 are stable, tetrahedral molecules with no lone pairs on the central atom for donation. PF3 (phosphorus trifluoride) has a lone pair on the phosphorus atom, making it capable of donating electrons and thus acting as a Lewis base (ligand).
Q179 | Inorganic Chemistry: Chemical Bonding Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following pairs of ions is isoelectronic and isostructural ?
(1) (1) SO₃²⁻, CO₃²⁻
(2) (2) ClO₃⁻, SO₃²⁻ ✓ Correct
(3) (3) CO₃²⁻, NO₃⁻
(4) (4) ClO₃⁻, CO₃²⁻
Solution: Isoelectronic means having the same number of electrons, and isostructural means having the same shape. For ClO₃⁻: Valence electrons = 7 (Cl) + 3×6 (O) + 1 (charge) = 26. VSEPR indicates AX3E (3 bonding pairs, 1 lone pair), so pyramidal shape. For SO₃²⁻: Valence electrons = 6 (S) + 3×6 (O) + 2 (charge) = 26. VSEPR indicates AX3E, so pyramidal shape. Thus, ClO₃⁻ and SO₃²⁻ are both isoelectronic and isostructural.
Q180 | Inorganic Chemistry: s-block Easy
ok conf:1.00
In context with beryllium, which one of the following statements is incorrect ?
(1) (1) Its salts rarely hydrolyze ✓ Correct
(2) (2) Its hydride is electron-deficient and polymeric
(3) (3) It is rendered passive by nitric acid
(4) (4) It forms Be₂C
Solution: Beryllium salts, due to the small size and high polarizing power of the Be²⁺ ion, are highly covalent and readily undergo hydrolysis. For example, BeCl2 hydrolyzes significantly in water to form Be(OH)2. Thus, the statement that its salts rarely hydrolyze is incorrect.
Q91 | Organisms and Populations Medium
ok conf:1.00
Gause's principle of competitive exclusion states that (1) More abundant species will exclude the less abundant species through competition. (2) Competition for the same resources excludes species having different food preferences. (3) No two species can occupy the same niche indefinitely for the same limited resources. (4) Larger organisms exclude smaller ones through competition.
(1) More abundant species will exclude the less abundant species through competition.
(2) Competition for the same resources excludes species having different food preferences.
(3) No two species can occupy the same niche indefinitely for the same limited resources. ✓ Correct
(4) Larger organisms exclude smaller ones through competition.
Solution: Gause's ‘Competitive Exclusion Principle' states that two closely related species competing for the same resources cannot coexist indefinitely and the competitively inferior one will be eliminated eventually.
Q92 | Biotechnology and its Applications Medium
ok conf:1.00
The two polypeptides of human insulin are linked together by (1) Hydrogen bonds (2) Phosphodiester bond (3) Covalent bond (4) Disulphide bridges
(1) Hydrogen bonds
(2) Phosphodiester bond
(3) Covalent bond
(4) Disulphide bridges ✓ Correct
Solution: The two polypeptides called A peptide and B peptide of human insulin were produced separately using plasmids of E.coli and were linked together by disulphide bonds in vitro.
Q93 | Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
The coconut water from tender coconut represents (1) Endocarp (2) Fleshy mesocarp (3) Free nuclear proembryo (4) Free nuclear endosperm
(1) Endocarp
(2) Fleshy mesocarp
(3) Free nuclear proembryo
(4) Free nuclear endosperm ✓ Correct
Solution: Endosperm development precedes embryo development. The most common type of endosperm development is free-nuclear endosperm where the PEN undergoes successive divisions without immediate cell wall formation after each division. Later on cell walls are laid between the nuclei turning it into cellular endosperm.
Q94 | Biological Classification Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following statements is wrong for viroids? (1) They lack a protein coat (2) They are smaller than viruses (3) They cause infections (4) Their RNA is of high molecular weight
(1) They lack a protein coat
(2) They are smaller than viruses
(3) They cause infections
(4) Their RNA is of high molecular weight ✓ Correct
Solution: The RNA of viroids has low molecular weight as it lacks a protein coat around it.
Q95 | Animal Kingdom Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following features is not present in the Phylum - Arthropoda? (1) Chitinous exoskeleton (2) Metameric segmentation (3) Parapodia (4) Jointed appendages
(1) Chitinous exoskeleton
(2) Metameric segmentation
(3) Parapodia ✓ Correct
(4) Jointed appendages
Solution: Parapodia are lateral appendages found in aquatic annelids which help in swimming.
Q96 | Principles of Inheritance and Variation Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following most appropriately describes haemophilia? (1) Recessive gene disorder (2) X - linked recessive gene disorder (3) Chromosomal disorder (4) Dominant gene disorder
(1) Recessive gene disorder
(2) X - linked recessive gene disorder ✓ Correct
(3) Chromosomal disorder
(4) Dominant gene disorder
Solution: Haemophilia is a sex linked recessive disease. The defective gene is present on the X chromosome of the 23rd pair but is recessive in nature. The heterozygous female (carrier) for haemophilia may transmit the disease to sons. The haemophilic male transmits the defective gene to the daughters.
Q97 | Photosynthesis Medium
ok conf:1.00
Emerson's enhancement effect and Red drop has been instrumental in the discovery of (1) Photophosphorylation and non-cyclic electron transport (2) Two photosystems operating simultaneously (3) Photophosphorylation and cyclic electron transport (4) Oxidative phosphorylation
(1) Photophosphorylation and non-cyclic electron transport
(2) Two photosystems operating simultaneously ✓ Correct
(3) Photophosphorylation and cyclic electron transport
(4) Oxidative phosphorylation
Solution: Emerson's enhancement effect is the increase in the rate of photosynthesis after the chloroplast were exposed to light of wavelength 680 nm and more than 680 nm (far red spectrum) simultaneously. This proved the existence of two photosystems in plants. He also proved that when the chloroplasts were exposed to only the monochromatic light of more than 680 nm (far red spectrum), there was a decrease in the photosynthetic rate which is known as red drop.
Q98 | Mineral Nutrition Medium
invalid_answer conf:1.00
In which of the following, all three are macronutrients? (1) Boron, zinc, manganese (2) Iron, copper, molybdenum (3) Molybdenum, magnesium, manganese (4) Nitrogen, nickel, phosphorus
(1) Boron, zinc, manganese
(2) Iron, copper, molybdenum
(3) Molybdenum, magnesium, manganese
(4) Nitrogen, nickel, phosphorus
Solution: In none of the groups all the elements are macronutrients.
Q99 | Breathing and Exchange of Gases Easy
ok conf:1.00
Name the chronic respiratory disorder caused mainly by cigarette smoking (1) Emphysema (2) Asthma (3) Respiratory acidosis (4) Respiratory alkalosis
(1) Emphysema ✓ Correct
(2) Asthma
(3) Respiratory acidosis
(4) Respiratory alkalosis
Solution: Emphysema is caused by excessive cigarette smoking. In this disorder the alveolar walls get damaged and become stiff, so the surface area for exchanges of gases reduces tremendously.
Q100 | Environmental Issues Easy
ok conf:1.00
A system of rotating crops with legume or grass pasture to improve soil structure and fertility is called (1) Ley farming (2) Contour farming (3) Strip farming (4) Shifting agriculture
(1) Ley farming ✓ Correct
(2) Contour farming
(3) Strip farming
(4) Shifting agriculture
Solution: Ley farming is used to improve soil structure in a natural way.
Q101 | Cell-The Unit of Life Easy
ok conf:1.00
Mitochondria and chloroplast are (a) semi-autonomous organelles (b) formed by division of pre-existing organelles and they contain DNA but lack protein synthesizing machinery. Which one of the following options is correct? (1) Both (a) and (b) are correct (2) (b) is true but (a) is false (3) (a) is true but (b) is false (4) Both (a) and (b) are false
(1) Both (a) and (b) are correct
(2) (b) is true but (a) is false
(3) (a) is true but (b) is false ✓ Correct
(4) Both (a) and (b) are false
Solution: 70 S ribosomes and RNA are present in both mitochondria and chloroplasts which are required for protein synthesis.
Q102 | Reproductive Health Easy
ok conf:1.00
In the context of Amniocentesis, which of the following statements is incorrect? (1) It is usually done when a woman is between 14-16 weeks pregnant. (2) It is used for prenatal sex determination (3) It can be used for detection of Down syndrome (4) It can be used for detection of Cleft palate
(1) It is usually done when a woman is between 14-16 weeks pregnant.
(2) It is used for prenatal sex determination
(3) It can be used for detection of Down syndrome
(4) It can be used for detection of Cleft palate ✓ Correct
Solution: Detection of Cleft palate can be done by ultrasound as early as 16 weeks into a pregnancy.
Q103 | Photosynthesis in Higher Plants Hard
ok conf:1.00
In a chloroplast the highest number of protons are found in (1) Stroma (2) Lumen of thylakoids (3) Inter membrane space (4) Antennae complex
(1) Stroma
(2) Lumen of thylakoids ✓ Correct
(3) Inter membrane space
(4) Antennae complex
Solution: Splitting of water molecules takes place on the inner side of the membrane so the protons of hydrogen ions produced by the splitting accumulate within the lumen of thylakoids.
Q104 | Neural Control and Coordination Medium
ok conf:1.00
Photosensitive compound in human eye is made up of (1) Guanosine and Retinol (2) Opsin and Retinal (3) Opsin and Retinol (4) Transducin and Retinene
(1) Guanosine and Retinol
(2) Opsin and Retinal ✓ Correct
(3) Opsin and Retinal
(4) Transducin and Retinene
Solution: Photosensitive compound in the human eye is made up of a purplish-red protein called rhodopsin which contains a derivative of Vitamin A. These are located on the outermost layer of the retina.
Q105 | Cell Cycle and Cell Division Easy
ok conf:1.00
Spindle fibres attach on to (1) Telomere of the chromosome (2) Kinetochore of the chromosome (3) Centromere of the chromosome (4) Kinetosome of the chromosome
(1) Telomere of the chromosome
(2) Kinetochore of the chromosome ✓ Correct
(3) Centromere of the chromosome
(4) Kinetosome of the chromosome
Solution: Kinetochores are small disc-like structures present on the surface of the centromere and these are the sites for the attachment of spindle fibres so that the chromatids can be pulled to opposite poles during anaphase stage.
Q106 | Animal Kingdom Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which is the National Aquatic Animal of India? (1) Gangetic shark (2) River dolphin (3) Blue whale (4) Seahorse
(1) Gangetic shark
(2) River dolphin ✓ Correct
(3) Blue whale
(4) Seahorse
Solution: To save the dolphins from extinction the government of India has declared the river dolphin, the national aquatic animal of India.
Q107 | Molecular Basis of Inheritance Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following is required as inducer(s) for the expression of Lac operon? (1) Glucose (2) Galactose (3) Lactose (4) Lactose and galactose
(1) Glucose
(2) Galactose
(3) Lactose ✓ Correct
(4) Lactose and galactose
Solution: The Lac operon in E. coli. bacteria (prokaryote) remain off but if lactose is added in its surrounding medium the operon turns on and starts metabolising lactose. Lac operon is inducible.
Q108 | Chemical Control and Coordination Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following pairs of hormones are not antagonistic (having opposite effects) to each other? (1) Parathormone – Calcitonin (2) Insulin – Glucagon (3) Aldosterone – Atrial Natriuretic Factor (4) Relaxin – Inhibin
(1) Parathormone – Calcitonin
(2) Insulin – Glucagon
(3) Aldosterone – Atrial Natriuretic Factor
(4) Relaxin – Inhibin ✓ Correct
Solution: Relaxin is a hormone which is secreted by the placenta, it relaxes the ligaments of the pelvis to ease partirution. While inhibin is secreted from the anterior pituitary, it decreases the secretion of FSH.
Q109 | Cell-The Unit of Life Easy
ok conf:1.00
Microtubules are the constituents of (1) Cilia, Flagella and Peroxisomes (2) Spindle fibres, Centrioles and Cilia (3) Centrioles, Spindle fibres and Chromatin (4) Centrosome, Nucleosome and Centrioles
(1) Cilia, Flagella and Peroxisomes
(2) Spindle fibres, Centrioles and Cilia ✓ Correct
(3) Centrioles, Spindle fibres and Chromatin
(4) Centrosome, Nucleosome and Centrioles
Solution: Microtubules are made up of tubulin proteins. Spindle fibres, centrioles and cilia have a considerable percentage of microtubules within them.
Q110 | Cell-The Unit of Life Easy
ok conf:1.00
A complex of ribosomes attached to a single strand of RNA is known as (1) Polysome (2) Polymer (3) Polypeptide (4) Okazaki fragment
(1) Polysome ✓ Correct
(2) Polymer
(3) Polypeptide
(4) Okazaki fragment
Solution: Polysomes are formed when two or more ribosomes are attached to a complex of mRNA molecules.
Q111 | Human Reproduction Easy
ok conf:1.00
Fertilization in humans is practically feasible only if (1) the sperms are transported into vagina just after the release of ovum in fallopian tube (2) the ovum and sperms are transported simultaneously to ampullary isthmic junction of the fallopian tube (3) the ovum and sperms are transported simultaneously to ampullary isthmic junction of the cervix (4) the sperms are transported into cervix within 48 hrs of release of ovum in uterus
(1) the sperms are transported into vagina just after the release of ovum in fallopian tube
(2) the ovum and sperms are transported simultaneously to ampullary isthmic junction of the fallopian tube ✓ Correct
(3) the ovum and sperms are transported simultaneously to ampullary isthmic junction of the cervix
(4) the sperms are transported into cervix within 48 hrs of release of ovum in uterus
Solution: The process of fusion of a sperm with an ovum is called fertilization and it occurs at the ampullary isthmic junction of the fallopian tube if sperm and ovum are transported simultaneously to this region.
Q112 | Breathing and Exchange of Gases Easy
ok conf:1.00
Asthma may be attributed to (1) bacterial infection of the lungs (2) allergic reaction of the mast cells in the lungs (3) inflammation of the trachea (4) accumulation of fluid in the lungs
(1) bacterial infection of the lungs
(2) allergic reaction of the mast cells in the lungs ✓ Correct
(3) inflammation of the trachea
(4) accumulation of fluid in the lungs
Solution: In asthma inflammation of bronchi and bronchioles occurs. It is a chronic problem so allergic reactions in the respiratory tract are more frequent which may lead to allergic reactions of the mast cells in the lungs.
Q113 | Plant Growth and Development Easy
ok conf:1.00
The Avena curvature is used for bioassay of (1) ABA (2) GA3 (3) IAA (4) Ethylene
(1) ABA
(2) GA3
(3) IAA ✓ Correct
(4) Ethylene
Solution: Bioassay is a quantitative method used to correlate the curvature obtained in a plant after the application of an external stimulus like light and the amount of growth substance produced endogenously.
Q114 | Morphology of Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
The standard petal of a papilionaceous corolla is also called (1) Carina (2) Pappus (3) Vexillum (4) Corona
(1) Carina
(2) Pappus
(3) Vexillum ✓ Correct
(4) Corona
Solution: The papilionaceous corolla are uneven in size and one of them is largest called vexillum.
Q115 | Morphology of Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
Tricarpellary, syncarpous, gynoecium is found in flowers of (1) Liliaceae (2) Solanaceae (3) Fabaceae (4) Poaceae
(1) Liliaceae ✓ Correct
(2) Solanaceae
(3) Fabaceae
(4) Poaceae
Solution: Family Liliaceae comprises monocot plants and monocots have trimerous flowers i.e., floral parts are multiple of three so is the carpel.
Q116 | Biological Classification Easy
ok conf:1.00
One of the major components of cell wall of most fungi is (1) Chitin (2) Peptidoglycan (3) Cellulose (4) Hemicellulose
(1) Chitin ✓ Correct
(2) Peptidoglycan
(3) Cellulose
(4) Hemicellulose
Solution: Fungal cell wall is made up of chitin, a complex polysaccharide which contains nitrogen. It is made of a linear polymer of N-acetyl-D-glucosamine monomers.
Q117 | Human Reproduction Easy
ok conf:1.00
Select the incorrect statement (1) FSH stimulates the sertoli cells which help in spermiogenesis (2) LH triggers ovulation in ovary (3) LH and FSH decrease gradually during the follicular phase (4) LH triggers secretion of androgens from the Leydig cells
(1) FSH stimulates the sertoli cells which help in spermiogenesis
(2) LH triggers ovulation in ovary
(3) LH and FSH decrease gradually during the follicular phase ✓ Correct
(4) LH triggers secretion of androgens from the Leydig cells
Solution: The reproductive cycle in the female primates is called menstrual cycle and it is repeated at the interval of 28 or 29 days. menstrual cycle is: During Proliferative phase also called follicular phase of menstrual cycle the endometrial lining start regenerating and, in the ovary, primary follicle grows and mature into Graafian follicle simultaneously, due to rise in gonadotropins (LH and FSH).
Q118 | Cell Cycle and Cell Division Easy
ok conf:1.00
In meiosis crossing over is initiated at (1) Pachytene (2) Leptotene (3) Zygotene (4) Diplotene
(1) Pachytene ✓ Correct
(2) Leptotene
(3) Zygotene
(4) Diplotene
Solution: Crossing over is a major event in meiosis cell division that results in variations. It occurs in the pachytene sub phase of meiosis-1.
Q119 | Principles of Inheritance Easy
ok conf:1.00
A tall true breeding garden pea plant is crossed with a dwarf true breeding garden pea plant. When the F1 plants were selfed the resulting genotypes were in the ratio of (1) 1:2:1:: Tall homozygous : Tall heterozygous: Dwarf (2) 1 : 2 : 1 :: Tall heterozygous : Tall homozygous : Dwarf (3) 3:1:: Tall : Dwarf (4) 3:1:: Dwarf : Tall
(1) 1:2:1:: Tall homozygous : Tall heterozygous: Dwarf ✓ Correct
(2) 1 : 2 : 1 :: Tall heterozygous : Tall homozygous : Dwarf
(3) 3:1:: Tall : Dwarf
(4) 3:1:: Dwarf : Tall
Solution: When selfing between F1 plants is done, gametes are formed. It was observed that a gamete carries either a recessive allele or a dominant allele but not both the alleles at the same time. Due to this reason, the law of segregation is known as the law of purity of gametes.
Q120 | Biodiversity and Conservation Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following is the most important cause of animals and plants being driven to extinction? (1) Over exploitation (2) Alien species invasion (3) Habitat loss and fragmentation (4) Co-extinctions
(1) Over exploitation
(2) Alien species invasion
(3) Habitat loss and fragmentation ✓ Correct
(4) Co-extinctions
Solution: The conversion of forests into agricultural land and building of dams or roads in a natural habitat results in its fragmentation; this has caused extinction of animals and plants.
Q121 | Ecosystem Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following is a characteristic feature of the cropland ecosystem? (1) Absence of soil organisms (2) Least genetic diversity (3) Absence of weeds (4) Ecological succession
(1) Absence of soil organisms
(2) Least genetic diversity ✓ Correct
(3) Absence of weeds
(4) Ecological succession
Solution: The Cropland ecosystem is a man-made ecosystem, and the cultivated crops belong to one or two plant species at a time. Hence, genetic diversity is least in such ecosystems.
Q122 | Human Reproduction Easy
ok conf:1.00
Changes in GnRH pulse frequency in females is controlled by circulating levels of (1) estrogen and progesterone (2) estrogen and inhibin (3) progesterone only (4) progesterone and inhibin
(1) estrogen and progesterone ✓ Correct
(2) estrogen and inhibin
(3) progesterone only
(4) progesterone and inhibin
Solution: The reproductive system is controlled by GnRH secretion from the brain which is under the influence of the amount of estrogen and progesterone is circulated. GnRH pulse frequency increases from one pulse every 90-100 min in the beginning of the follicular phase which increases in the late follicular phase.
Q123 | Biotechnology-Principles and Processes Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following is not a feature of the plasmids? (1) Independent replication (2) Circular structure (3) Transferable (4) Single - stranded
(1) Independent replication
(2) Circular structure
(3) Transferable
(4) Single - stranded ✓ Correct
Solution: Plasmids are found in bacterial cells independent of the control of chromosomal DNA. It is a single circular strand of DNA.
Q124 | Structural Organisation in Animals Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following features is not present in Periplaneta americana? (1) Schizocoelom as body cavity (2) Indeterminate and radial cleavage during embryonic development (3) Exoskeleton composed of N-acetylglucosamine (4) Metamerically segmented body
(1) Schizocoelom as body cavity
(2) Indeterminate and radial cleavage during embryonic development ✓ Correct
(3) Exoskeleton composed of N-acetylglucosamine
(4) Metamerically segmented body
Solution: Indeterminate and radial cleavage during embryonic development is a feature of vertebrates and certain invertebrates like echinoderms.
Q125 | Human Health and Diseases Easy
ok conf:1.00
In higher vertebrates, the immune system can distinguish self-cells and non-self. If this property is lost due to genetic abnormality and it attacks self-cells, then it leads to (1) Allergic response (2) Graft rejection (3) Auto-immune disease (4) Active immunity
(1) Allergic response
(2) Graft rejection
(3) Auto-immune disease ✓ Correct
(4) Active immunity
Solution: In auto-immune disease the immune system mistakes the body cells as foreign and starts attacking them.
Q126 | Principles of Inheritance Easy
invalid_answer conf:1.00
Match the terms in Column-1 with their description in Column-Il and choose the correct option. Column-1 (a) Dominance (b) Codominance (c) Pleiotropy (d) Polygenic inheritance Column-II (i) Many genes govern a single character (ii) In a heterozygous organism only one allele expresses itself (iii) In a heterozygous organism both alleles express themselves fully (iv) A single gene influences many characters (1) (a)-(ii), (b)-(ii), (c)-(i), (d)-(iii) (2) (a)-(ii), (b)-(ii), (c)-(iv), (d)-(iii) (3) (a)-(iv), (b)-(iii), (c)-(ii), (d)-(iv) (4) (a)-(iv), (b)-(iii), (c)-(i), (d)-(ii)
(1) (a)-(ii), (b)-(ii), (c)-(i), (d)-(iii)
(2) (a)-(ii), (b)-(ii), (c)-(iv), (d)-(iii)
(3) (a)-(iv), (b)-(iii), (c)-(ii), (d)-(iv)
(4) (a)-(iv), (b)-(iii), (c)-(i), (d)-(ii)
Solution: Dominance - In a heterozygous organism only one allele expresses itself. Co-dominance - In a heterozygous organism both alleles express themselves fully. Pleiotropy - A single gene influences many characters. Polygenic inheritance - Many genes govern a single character. (Correct matching: (a)-(ii), (b)-(iii), (c)-(iv), (d)-(i). None of the options match this exact mapping, hence marked as 'none' in source.)
Q127 | Environmental Issues Easy
ok conf:1.00
Joint Forest Management Concept was introduced in India during (1) 1960 s (2) 1970 s (3) 1980 s (4) 1990 s
(1) 1960 s
(2) 1970 s
(3) 1980 s ✓ Correct
(4) 1990 s
Solution: When the government of India realised the participation of local communities in conserving forests then in the 1980s the Joint Forest Management Concept was introduced.
Q128 | Principles of Inheritance Medium
ok conf:1.00
Pick out the correct statements. (a) Haemophilia is a sex-linked recessive disease (b) Down's syndrome is due to aneuploidy (c) Phenylketonuria is an autosomal recessive gene disorder. (d) Sickle cell anaemia is a X-linked recessive gene disorder (1) (a) and (d) are correct (2) (b) and (d) are correct (3) (a), (c) and (d) are correct (4) (a), (b) and (c) are correct
(1) (a) and (d) are correct
(2) (b) and (d) are correct
(3) (a), (c) and (d) are correct
(4) (a), (b) and (c) are correct ✓ Correct
Solution: Sickle cell anaemia is an autosome linked recessive trait. (Statements (a), (b), (c) are correct, (d) is incorrect as Sickle cell anaemia is an autosomal recessive gene disorder, not X-linked. Therefore, (a), (b) and (c) are correct statements.)
Q129 | Biological Classification Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following statements is wrong? (1) Cyanobacteria are also called blue-green algae (2) Golden algae are also called desmids (3) Eubacteria are also called false bacteria. (4) Phycomycetes are also called algal fungi
(1) Cyanobacteria are also called blue-green algae
(2) Golden algae are also called desmids
(3) Eubacteria are also called false bacteria. ✓ Correct
(4) Phycomycetes are also called algal fungi
Solution: In latin ‘eu' means 'true'. Therefore, eubacteria means true bacteria. (Statement (3) is wrong because Eubacteria are true bacteria, not false bacteria.)
Q130 | Reproduction in Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
Proximal end of the filament of stamen is attached to the (1) Anther (2) Connective (3) Placenta (4) Thalamus or petal
(1) Anther
(2) Connective
(3) Placenta
(4) Thalamus or petal ✓ Correct
Solution: Proximal end means the basal part of the filament and it remains attached to the thalamus.
Q131 | Reproductive Health Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following approaches does not give the defined action of contraceptive? (1) Barrier methods - Prevent fertilization (2) Intrauterine devices - Increase phagocytosis of sperms, suppress sperm motility and fertilizing capacity of sperms (3) Hormonal contraceptives - Prevent/retard entry of sperms, prevent ovulation and fertilization (4) Vasectomy - Prevents spermatogenesis
(1) Barrier methods - Prevent fertilization
(2) Intrauterine devices - Increase phagocytosis of sperms, suppress sperm motility and fertilizing capacity of sperms
(3) Hormonal contraceptives - Prevent/retard entry of sperms, prevent ovulation and fertilization
(4) Vasectomy - Prevents spermatogenesis ✓ Correct
Solution: Vasectomy is cutting and ligating both the vas deferens in males to block the gamete transport.
Q132 | Biotechnology – Principles and Processes Easy
ok conf:1.00
The taq polymerase enzyme is obtained from (1) Thermus aquaticus (2) Thiobacillus ferrooxidans (3) Bacillus subtilis (4) Pseudomonas putida
(1) Thermus aquaticus ✓ Correct
(2) Thiobacillus ferrooxidans
(3) Bacillus subtilis
(4) Pseudomonas putida
Solution: Thermostable DNA polymerase enzyme is obtained from a bacterium Thermus aquaticus. This enzyme remains active at high temperature and is of great significance in PCR.
Q133 | Human Reproduction Medium
ok conf:1.00
Identify the correct statement on 'inhibin'. (1) Inhibits the secretion of LH, FSH and Prolactin. (2) Is produced by granulosa cells in ovary and inhibits the secretion of FSH. (3) Is produced by granulosa cells in ovary and inhibits the secretion of LH. (4) Is produced by nurse cells in testes and inhibits the secretion of LH.
(1) Inhibits the secretion of LH, FSH and Prolactin.
(2) Is produced by granulosa cells in ovary and inhibits the secretion of FSH. ✓ Correct
(3) Is produced by granulosa cells in ovary and inhibits the secretion of LH.
(4) Is produced by nurse cells in testes and inhibits the secretion of LH.
Solution: Inhibin hormone in women inhibits the secretion of FSH and reduces the hypothalamic LH.
Q134 | Biotechnology and its Applications Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which part of the tobacco plant is infected by Meloidogyne incognita? (1) Flower (2) Leaf (3) Stem (4) Root
(1) Flower
(2) Leaf
(3) Stem
(4) Root ✓ Correct
Solution: Meloidogyne incognita, is a nematode which infects the roots of tobacco plants.
Q135 | Human Health and Diseases Easy
ok conf:1.00
Anti-venom injection contains preformed antibodies while polio drops that are administered into the body contain (1) Activated pathogens (2) Harvested antibodies (3) Gamma globulin (4) Attenuated pathogens
(1) Activated pathogens
(2) Harvested antibodies
(3) Gamma globulin
(4) Attenuated pathogens ✓ Correct
Solution: In the form of polio drops, our body is exposed to weak pathogens and to fight against it antibodies are produced. This type of immunity is called active immunity.
Q136 | Cell - The Unit of Life Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following cell organelles is enclosed by a single membrane? (1) Mitochondria (2) Chloroplasts (3) Lysosomes (4) Nuclei
(1) Mitochondria
(2) Chloroplasts
(3) Lysosomes ✓ Correct
(4) Nuclei
Solution: Lysosomes are enclosed by a single membrane.
Q137 | Locomotion and Movement Medium
ok conf:1.00
Lack of relaxation between successive stimuli in sustained muscle contraction is known as (1) Spasm (2) Fatigue (3) Tetanus (4) Tonus
(1) Spasm
(2) Fatigue
(3) Tetanus ✓ Correct
(4) Tonus
Solution: Tetanus is a bacterial disease characterized by muscle spasm. There is lack of relaxation between successive stimuli in sustained muscle contraction.
Q138 | Morphology of Flowering Plants Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following is not a stem modification? (1) Pitcher of Nepenthes (2) Thorns of citrus (3) Tendrils of cucumber (4) Flattened structures of Opuntia
(1) Pitcher of Nepenthes ✓ Correct
(2) Thorns of citrus
(3) Tendrils of cucumber
(4) Flattened structures of Opuntia
Solution: Pitcher of Nepenthes, is a modification of a leaf.
Q139 | Cell - The Unit of Life Medium
ok conf:1.00
Water soluble pigments found in plant cell vacuoles are (1) Xanthophylls (2) Chlorophylls (3) Carotenoids (4) Anthocyanins
(1) Xanthophylls
(2) Chlorophylls
(3) Carotenoids
(4) Anthocyanins ✓ Correct
Solution: A water-soluble pigment, present in sap vacuole of plants is anthocyanin.
Q140 | Plant Kingdom Medium
ok conf:1.00
Select the correct statement. (1) Gymnosperms are both homosporous and heterosporous (2) Salvinia, Ginkgo and Pinus all are gymnosperms (3) Sequoia is one of the tallest trees (4) The leaves of gymnosperms are not well adapted to extremes of climate
(1) Gymnosperms are both homosporous and heterosporous
(2) Salvinia, Ginkgo and Pinus all are gymnosperms
(3) Sequoia is one of the tallest trees ✓ Correct
(4) The leaves of gymnosperms are not well adapted to extremes of climate
Solution: Sequoia is one of the tallest gymnosperms.
Q141 | Molecular Basis of Inheritance Hard
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following is not required for any of the techniques of DNA fingerprinting available at present?
(1) Polymerase chain reaction
(2) Zinc finger analysis ✓ Correct
(3) Restriction enzymes
(4) DNA-DNA hybridization
Solution: Zinc finger is a small protein structural motif that is characterized by the coordination of one or more zinc ions (Zn) in order to stabilize the fold. It is not used in DNA fingerprinting techniques.
Q142 | Structural Organisation in Animals Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which type of tissue correctly matches with its location? Tissue (1) Smooth muscle (2) Areolar tissue (3) Transitional epithelium (4) Cuboidal epithelium Location Wall of intestine Tendons Tip nose Lining of stomach
(1) Smooth muscle - Wall of intestine ✓ Correct
(2) Areolar tissue - Tendons
(3) Transitional epithelium - Tip nose
(4) Cuboidal epithelium - Lining of stomach
Solution: Smooth muscles are found in the wall of intestine, stomach, and other visceral organs. Smooth muscles are involuntary in nature and are present in all those organs which are not under our conscious control.
Q143 | Photosynthesis in Higher Plants Medium
ok conf:1.00
A plant in your garden avoids photorespiratory losses, has improved water use efficiency, shows high rates of photosynthesis at high temperatures and has improved efficiency of nitrogen utilisation. In which of the following physiological groups would you assign this plant? (1) C3 (2) C4 (3) CAM (4) Nitrogen fixer
(1) C3
(2) C4 ✓ Correct
(3) CAM
(4) Nitrogen fixer
Solution: C4 plants have improved water using efficiency, high rate of photosynthesis at high temperatures and nitrogen utilisation efficiency.
Q144 | Evolution Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following structures is homologous to the wing of a bird? (1) Dorsal fin of a Shark (2) Wing of a Moth (3) Hind limb of Rabbit (4) Flipper of Whale
(1) Dorsal fin of a Shark
(2) Wing of a Moth
(3) Hind limb of Rabbit
(4) Flipper of Whale ✓ Correct
Solution: Wings of a bird and flipper of a whale have the same origin but different functions. Hence, they are homologous organs.
Q145 | Animal Kingdom Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following characteristic features always holds true for the corresponding group of animals? (1) Cartilaginous endoskeleton - Chondrichthyes (2) Viviparous - Mammalia (3) Possess a mouth with an upper and a lower jaw - Chordata (4) 3 - chambered heart with one incompletely divided ventricle - Reptilia
(1) Cartilaginous endoskeleton - Chondrichthyes ✓ Correct
[option 1]

(2) Viviparous - Mammalia
(3) Possess a mouth with an upper and a lower jaw - Chordata
[option 3]

(4) 3 - chambered heart with one incompletely divided ventricle - Reptilia
[option 4]

Solution: Chondrichthyes have cartilaginous endo-skeletons.
[solution diagram]

Q146 | Human Health and Diseases Medium
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following statements is not true for cancer cells in relation to mutations? (1) Mutations in proto-oncogenes accelerate the cell cycle. (2) Mutations destroy telomerase inhibitors. (3) Mutations inactive the cell control. (4) Mutations inhibit production of telomerase.
(1) Mutations in proto-oncogenes accelerate the cell cycle.
(2) Mutations destroy telomerase inhibitors.
(3) Mutations inactive the cell control.
(4) Mutations inhibit production of telomerase. ✓ Correct
Solution: Telomerase is required during DNA replication. Cancer cells undergo repeated DNA replication so the telomerase production cannot be inhibited.
Q147 | Chemical Control and Coordination Medium
ok conf:1.00
The amino acid Tryptophan is the precursor for the synthesis of (1) Melatonin and Serotonin (2) Thyroxine and Triiodothyronine (3) Estrogen and Progesterone (4) Cortisol and Cortisone
(1) Melatonin and Serotonin ✓ Correct
(2) Thyroxine and Triiodothyronine
(3) Estrogen and Progesterone
(4) Cortisol and Cortisone
Solution: Tryptophan is one of the essential amino acids and the precursor of melatonin and serotonin hormones. Melatonin regulates circadian rhythms and serotonin stabilizes mood, feelings like happiness and well being.
Q148 | Evolution Medium
ok conf:1.00
Following are the two statements regarding the origin of life. (a) The earliest organisms that appeared on the earth were non-green and presumably anaerobes. (b) The first autotrophic organisms were the chemoautotrophs that never released oxygen. Of the above statements, which one of the following options is correct? (1) (a) is correct but (b) is false. (2) (b) is correct but (a) is false. (3) Both (a) and (b) are correct. (4) Both (a) and (b) are false.
(1) (a) is correct but (b) is false.
(2) (b) is correct but (a) is false.
(3) Both (a) and (b) are correct. ✓ Correct
(4) Both (a) and (b) are false.
Solution: Coacervates are the protobionts which are believed to give rise to earliest organisms that were non-photosynthetic and anaerobic. Eventually bacteria like organisms which were chemoautotrophs came into existence. Chemoautotrophs did not use water for fetching hydrogen like green bacteria so never released oxygen.
Q149 | Breathing and Exchange of Gases Medium
ok conf:1.00
Reduction in pH of blood will (1) reduce the rate of heartbeat. (2) reduce the blood supply to the brain. (3) decrease the affinity of haemoglobin with oxygen. (4) release bicarbonate ions by the liver.
(1) reduce the rate of heartbeat.
(2) reduce the blood supply to the brain.
(3) decrease the affinity of haemoglobin with oxygen. ✓ Correct
(4) release bicarbonate ions by the liver.
Solution: Haemoglobin is the respiratory pigment in the human body which has the affinity to bind with oxygen and carbon dioxide. Haemoglobin's oxygen binding affinity is inversely related to both pH of blood and concentration of carbon dioxide. Carbon dioxide reacts with water to form carbonic acid and an increase in CO2 level in the blood results in decreased pH of blood. This triggers the haemoglobin in releasing its load of oxygen.
Q150 | Evolution Easy
ok conf:1.00
Analogous structures are a result of (1) Divergent evolution (2) Convergent evolution (3) Shared ancestry (4) Stabilizing selection
(1) Divergent evolution
(2) Convergent evolution ✓ Correct
(3) Shared ancestry
(4) Stabilizing selection
Solution: Structures that are morphologically similar but not anatomically are called analogous structures. Such a type of structure helps diverse life forms to thrive in a common habitat. Thus, analogy supports convergent evolution.
Q151 | Biotechnology – Principles and Processes Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following is a restriction endonuclease? (1) Hind II (2) Protease (3) DNase I (4) RNase
(1) Hind II ✓ Correct
[option 1]

(2) Protease
[option 2]

(3) DNase I
[option 3]

(4) RNase
[option 4]

Solution: The first restriction endonuclease is Hind II and it always cuts DNA molecules at a specific sequence of six base pairs. This specific sequence is known as the recognition sequence.
[solution diagram]

Q152 | Ecology Easy
ok conf:1.00
The term ecosystem was coined by (1) E.P. Odum (2) A.G. Tansley (3) E. Haeckel (4) E. Warming
(1) E.P. Odum
(2) A.G. Tansley ✓ Correct
(3) E. Haeckel
(4) E. Warming
Solution: Sir Arthur George Tansley, a pioneer in the science of ecology coined the term ecosystem.
Q153 | Biomolecules Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following statements is wrong? (1) Sucrose is a disaccharide. (2) Cellulose is a polysaccharide. (3) Uracil is a pyrimidine. (4) Glycine is a sulphur containing amino acid.
(1) Sucrose is a disaccharide.
(2) Cellulose is a polysaccharide.
(3) Uracil is a pyrimidine.
(4) Glycine is a sulphur containing amino acid. ✓ Correct
Solution: Glycine is a neutral amino acid. It has two hydrogen atoms attached to the alpha carbon besides one carboxylic group and one amino group.
Q154 | Plant Kingdom Easy
ok conf:1.00
In bryophytes and pteridophytes, transport of male gametes requires (1) Wind (2) Insects (3) Birds (4) Water
(1) Wind
(2) Insects
(3) Birds
(4) Water ✓ Correct
Solution: Bryophytes and pteridophytes produce flagellated male gametes and they need water to reach the archegonium.
Q155 | Organisms and Populations Medium
ok conf:1.00
When does the growth rate of a population following the logistic model equal zero? The logistic model is given as dN/dt = rN (1−N/K): (1) when N/K is exactly one. (2) when N nears the carrying capacity of the habitat. (3) when N/K equals zero. (4) when the death rate is greater than the birth rate.
(1) when N/K is exactly one. ✓ Correct
(2) when N nears the carrying capacity of the habitat.
(3) when N/K equals zero.
(4) when the death rate is greater than the birth rate.
Solution: In logistic model of population growth N = Population density at time t K = Carrying capacity In the equation, dN/dt = rN (1–N/K) if K is much larger than N, the population can grow quickly; if K is smaller than N, the population will shrink. If K = N, the population will remain the same over time.
Q156 | Reproduction in Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following statements is not true? (1) Tapetum helps in the dehiscence of anther (2) Exine of pollen grains is made up of sporopollenin (3) Pollen grains of many species cause severe allergies (4) Stored pollen in liquid nitrogen can be used in the crop breeding programmes
(1) Tapetum helps in the dehiscence of anther ✓ Correct
(2) Exine of pollen grains is made up of sporopollenin
(3) Pollen grains of many species cause severe allergies
(4) Stored pollen in liquid nitrogen can be used in the crop breeding programmes
Solution: Tapetum nourishes the developing pollen grains.
Q157 | Ecosystem Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following would appear as the pioneer organisms on bare rocks? (1) Lichens (2) Liverworts (3) Mosses (4) Green algae
(1) Lichens ✓ Correct
(2) Liverworts
(3) Mosses
(4) Green algae
Solution: The species that invade any bare, lifeless habitat is called a pioneer species. In xerarch succession lichens are the pioneer organisms as these secrete enzymes that dissolve the rocks and pay way for the other plants to colonise the habitat.
Q158 | Molecular Basis of Inheritance Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following is the starter codon? (1) AUG (2) UGA (3) UAA (4) UAG
(1) AUG ✓ Correct
(2) UGA
(3) UAA
(4) UAG
Solution: The translation unit in mRNA is a sequence of RNA that has AUG (start codon) at its 5’ end and some additional UTRs for efficient translation process and in the same way at the 3'end stop codon is present, after its UTRs.
Q159 | Animal Kingdom Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which one of the following characteristics is not shared by birds and mammals? (1) Ossified endoskeleton (2) Breathing using lungs (3) Viviparity (4) Warm blooded nature
(1) Ossified endoskeleton
(2) Breathing using lungs
(3) Viviparity ✓ Correct
(4) Warm blooded nature
Solution: Birds are oviparous while mammals are viviparous.
Q160 | The Living World Easy
ok conf:1.00
Nomenclature is governed by certain universal rules. Which one of the following is contrary to the rules of nomenclature? (1) Biological names can be written in any language (2) The first word in a biological name represents the genus name, and the second is a specific epithet (3) The names are written in Latin and are italicised (4) When written by hand, the names are to be underlined
(1) Biological names can be written in any language ✓ Correct
(2) The first word in a biological name represents the genus name, and the second is a specific epithet
(3) The names are written in Latin and are italicised
(4) When written by hand, the names are to be underlined
Solution: Biological names are written in Latin. It is one of the rules according to Binomial Nomenclature which is widely accepted by the biologists.
Q161 | Circulation of Body Fluids Easy
ok conf:1.00
Blood pressure in the pulmonary artery is (1) same as that in the aorta. (2) more than that in the carotid. (3) more than that in the pulmonary vein. (4) less than that in the vena cavae.
(1) same as that in the aorta.
(2) more than that in the carotid.
(3) more than that in the pulmonary vein. ✓ Correct
(4) less than that in the vena cavae.
Solution: In veins the blood always flows under low blood pressure in comparison to arteries.
Q162 | Morphology of Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
Cotyledon of maize grain is called (1) plumule (2) coleorhiza (3) coleoptile (4) scutellum
(1) plumule
(2) coleorhiza
(3) coleoptile
(4) scutellum ✓ Correct
Solution: In monocot seed there is only one cotyledon called scutellum.
Q163 | Digestion and Absorption Easy
ok conf:1.00
In the stomach, gastric acid is secreted by the (1) gastrin secreting cells (2) parietal cells (3) peptic cells (4) acidic cells
(1) gastrin secreting cells
(2) parietal cells ✓ Correct
(3) peptic cells
(4) acidic cells
Solution: In the gastric mucosa numerous gastric glands are present among them parietal cells secrete mucus.
Q164 | Environmental Issues Easy
ok conf:1.00
Depletion of which gas in the atmosphere can lead to an increased incidence of skin cancers (1) Nitrous oxide (2) Ozone (3) Ammonia (4) Methane
(1) Nitrous oxide
(2) Ozone ✓ Correct
(3) Ammonia
(4) Methane
Solution: UV radiations of wavelengths shorter than UV-B are absorbed by the Earth's Atmosphere considering that the ozone layer is intact. But if the ozone layer gets depleted then the UV-B radiation damages the skin cells and various types of skin cancers can occur.
Q165 | Biological Classification Easy
ok conf:1.00
Chrysophytes, Euglenoids, Dinoflagellates and Slime moulds are included in the kingdom (1) Monera (2) Protista (3) Fungi (4) Animalia
(1) Monera
(2) Protista ✓ Correct
(3) Fungi
(4) Animalia
Solution: Chrysophytes include golden algae (desmids) and diatoms.
Q166 | Photosynthesis Easy
ok conf:1.00
Water vapour comes out from the plant leaf through the stomatal opening. Through the same stomatal opening carbon dioxide diffuses into the plant during photosynthesis. Reason out the above statements using one of the following options. (1) Both processes cannot happen simultaneously. (2) Both processes can happen together because the diffusion coefficient of water and CO2 is different. (3) The above processes happen only during night-time. (4) One process occurs during daytime, and the other at night.
(1) Both processes cannot happen simultaneously.
(2) Both processes can happen together because the diffusion coefficient of water and CO2 is different. ✓ Correct
(3) The above processes happen only during night-time.
(4) One process occurs during daytime, and the other at night.
Solution: Gaseous exchange and transpiration occur simultaneously. Diffusion coefficient of carbon dioxide is less in comparison to water vapour so both the gases can diffuse simultaneously.
Q167 | Excretion and Osmoregulation Medium
ok conf:1.00
In mammals, which blood vessel would normally carry the largest amount of urea? (1) Renal Vein (2) Dorsal Aorta (3) Hepatic Vein (4) Hepatic Portal Vein
(1) Renal Vein
(2) Dorsal Aorta
(3) Hepatic Vein ✓ Correct
(4) Hepatic Portal Vein
Solution: The liver is the primary site for the breakdown of excess amino acids into urea. Hence, the hepatic vein would normally carry more urea.
Q168 | Reproduction in Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
Seed formation without fertilization in flowering plants involves the process of (1) Sporulation (2) Budding (3) Somatic hybridization (4) Apomixis
(1) Sporulation
(2) Budding
(3) Somatic hybridization
(4) Apomixis ✓ Correct
Solution: In few flowering plants the egg cell is developed without reduction division so is diploid and it develops into an embryo without fertilization. Such seeds are diploid and are called apomictic seeds. So apomixis is a form of asexual reproduction.
Q169 | Microbes in Human Welfare Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following is wrongly matched in the given table? Microbe Product (1) Trichoderma polysporum - Cyclosporine A,immunsuppressive drug (2) Monascus purpureus - Statins, Lowering of blood cholesterol (3) Streptococcus - Streptokinase, Removal of clot from blood vessel (4) Clostridium butylicum - Lipase, Removal of oil stains
(1) Trichoderma polysporum - Cyclosporine A,immunsuppressive drug
(2) Monascus purpureus - Statins, Lowering of blood cholesterol
(3) Streptococcus - Streptokinase, Removal of clot from blood vessel
(4) Clostridium butylicum - Lipase, Removal of oil stains ✓ Correct
Solution: Clostridium butylicum is a bacterium used to obtain butyric acid.
Q170 | Principles of Inheritance and Variations Easy
ok conf:1.00
In a testcross involving F₁ dihybrid flies, more parental-type offspring were produced than the recombinant-type offspring. This indicates. (1) The two genes are located on two different chromosomes. (2) Chromosomes failed to separate during meiosis. (3) The two genes are linked and present on the same chromosome. (4) Both of the characters are controlled by more than one gene.
(1) The two genes are located on two different chromosomes.
(2) Chromosomes failed to separate during meiosis.
(3) The two genes are linked and present on the same chromosome. ✓ Correct
(4) Both of the characters are controlled by more than one gene.
Solution: Genes are present on the chromosomes in a single linear row. Distance between genes varies and when the distance between two or more genes is less then they tend to move together during gamete formation and do not separate even during crossing over. This phenomenon is called linkage which reduces the chances of recombination and more parental types of offspring are produced.
Q171 | Evolution Easy
ok conf:1.00
It is much easier for a small animal to run uphill than for a large animal, because (1) It is easier to carry a small body weight. (2) Smaller animals have a higher metabolic rate. (3) Small animals have a lower O2 requirement. (4) The efficiency of muscles in large animals is less than in small animals.
(1) It is easier to carry a small body weight.
(2) Smaller animals have a higher metabolic rate. ✓ Correct
(3) Small animals have a lower O2 requirement.
(4) The efficiency of muscles in large animals is less than in small animals.
Solution: For faster movement lots of energy is required and to achieve that amount of energy the metabolic rate needs to be faster. That is why smaller animals can easily run uphill.
Q172 | Cell Cycle and Cell Division Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following is not a characteristic feature during mitosis in somatic cells? (1) Spindle fibres (2) Disappearance of nucleolus (3) Chromosome movement (4) Synapsis
(1) Spindle fibres
(2) Disappearance of nucleolus
(3) Chromosome movement
(4) Synapsis ✓ Correct
Solution: Synapsis is a feature that occurs during meiosis cell division.
Q173 | Reproduction in Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following statements is not correct? (1) Pollen grains of many species can germinate on the stigma of a flower, but only one pollen tube of the same species grows into the style. (2) Insects that consume pollen or nectar without bringing about pollination are called pollen/nectar robbers. (3) Pollen germination and pollen tube growth are regulated by chemical components of pollen interacting with those of the pistil. (4) Some reptiles have also been reported as pollinators in some plant species.
(1) Pollen grains of many species can germinate on the stigma of a flower, but only one pollen tube of the same species grows into the style. ✓ Correct
(2) Insects that consume pollen or nectar without bringing about pollination are called pollen/nectar robbers.
(3) Pollen germination and pollen tube growth are regulated by chemical components of pollen interacting with those of the pistil.
(4) Some reptiles have also been reported as pollinators in some plant species.
Solution: Pollen germination is species specific and all the pollen grains which belong to that species can germinate on the stigma but the pollen tube which grows faster and reaches the micropyle first is able to release the male gametes into the female gametophyte.
Q174 | Anatomy of Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
Specialised epidermal cells surrounding the guard cells are called (1) Complementary cells (2) Subsidiary cells (3) Bulliform cells (4) Lenticels
(1) Complementary cells
(2) Subsidiary cells ✓ Correct
(3) Bulliform cells
(4) Lenticels
Solution: The stomata are surrounded usually by two guard cells which are further surrounded by subsidiary cells. All these cells help in the functioning of stomata.
Q175 | Digestion and Absorption Easy
ok conf:1.00
Which of the following guards the opening of the hepato-pancreatic duct into the duodenum? (1) Semilunar valve (2) Ileocaecal valve (3) Pyloric sphincter (4) Sphincter of Oddi
(1) Semilunar valve
(2) Ileocaecal valve
(3) Pyloric sphincter
(4) Sphincter of Oddi ✓ Correct
Solution: The bile duct and the pancreatic duct open together into the duodenum as the common hepato-pancreatic duct which is guarded by a sphincter called sphincter of Oddi.
Q176 | Morphology of Flowering Plants Easy
ok conf:1.00
Stems modified into flat green organs performing the functions of leaves are known as (1) Cladodes (2) Phyllodes (3) Phylloclades (4) Scales
(1) Cladodes
(2) Phyllodes
(3) Phylloclades ✓ Correct
(4) Scales
Solution: In cactus the leaves are converted into spines to reduce transpiration, so the stem becomes green, flat, leaf-like and performs photosynthesis. Such a stem is called phylloclade.
Q177 | Biological Classification Easy
ok conf:1.00
The primitive prokaryotes responsible for the production of biogas from the dung of ruminant animals, include the (1) Halophiles (2) Thermoacidophiles (3) Methanogens (4) Eubacteria
(1) Halophiles
(2) Thermoacidophiles
(3) Methanogens ✓ Correct
(4) Eubacteria
Solution: Methanogens are archaebacteria which are specialised to survive in most harsh habitats. These methanogens are used to produce methane, a component of biogas in biogas plants.
Q178 | Environmental Issues Easy
ok conf:1.00
A river with an inflow of domestic sewage rich inorganic waste may result in (1) Drying of the river very soon due to algal bloom. (2) Increased population of aquatic food web organisms. (3) An increased production of fish due to biodegradable nutrients. (4) Death of fish due to lack of oxygen.
(1) Drying of the river very soon due to algal bloom.
(2) Increased population of aquatic food web organisms.
(3) An increased production of fish due to biodegradable nutrients.
(4) Death of fish due to lack of oxygen. ✓ Correct
Solution: At the site of sewage discharge into a river the amount of dissolved oxygen is reduced tremendously due to rise in biochemical oxygen demand, this results in the death of fish and other aquatic animals.
Q179 | Principles of Inheritance and Variations Medium
ok conf:1.00
A cell at the telophase stage is observed by a student in a plant brought from the field. He tells his teacher that this cell is not like other cells at telophase stage. There is no formation of cell plates and thus the cell contains a greater number of chromosomes as compared to other dividing cells. This would result in (1) Aneuploidy (2) Polyploidy (3) Somaclonal variation (4) Polyteny
(1) Aneuploidy
(2) Polyploidy ✓ Correct
(3) Somaclonal variation
(4) Polyteny
Solution: Polyploidy means increase in a whole set of chromosomes i.e., Triticum aestivum is an allohexaploid wheat variety. Polyploidy is often seen in plants.
Q180 | Biomolecules Easy
ok conf:1.00
A typical fat molecule is made up of (1) Three glycerol molecules and one fatty acid molecule (2) One glycerol and three fatty acid molecules (3) One glycerol and one fatty acid molecule (4) Three glycerol and three fatty acid molecules
(1) Three glycerol molecules and one fatty acid molecule
(2) One glycerol and three fatty acid molecules ✓ Correct
(3) One glycerol and one fatty acid molecule
(4) Three glycerol and three fatty acid molecules
Solution: In a typical fat molecule three fatty acids are found esterified with glycerol.
